title: I Live in Lifelong Financial Ease
subtitle: An affirmation for anchoring ease as a permanent financial baseline, not a temporary good phase
excerpt: A brief affirmation from Day 70 of a 12-week money program. The statement names ease as a permanent condition rather than a result of circumstances — something anchored by the practice work done, not dependent on everything going right at once.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 70 reframe theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use at the end of a significant period of money work when you want to mark the shift from 'getting better with money' to 'this is now who I am with money'.

content.text: A brief affirmation from Day 70 of a 12-week money program — the completion point of Week 10. The statement names ease not as a phase that might end, but as something anchored in the body through sustained practice. The difference between 'I am doing well with money right now' and 'financial ease is my permanent baseline' is what this affirmation is working with.
Say it three times, slowly. The third line is the most important: ease as something you carry forward, not something you maintain by staying vigilant.
I live in lifelong financial ease. Money flows steadily and I trust it completely. I carry this calm into every future year.
Adapted from the Day 70 reframe theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
